sections.paragraph:
m. wicker-work, woven of twigs or rods, from the root כָּלַב No. II., specially-

(1) a basket for fruit, Amo 8:1.

(2) a cage for birds, Jer 5:27. (Syr. ܟܠܘܽܒܐܳ id., and the same word is also adopted in Greek, κλωβός, κλουβός, κλοβός, a cage, see Bochart, Hieroz. i. 662, ii. p. 90). It is also pr.n. m. [Chelub].-(a) 1Ch 4:11.-(b) 1Ch 27:26.
